mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals impending recession risk, which triggers earnings downgrades and risk-off positioning, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the UNRATE spike, or forward returns do not differ significantly from non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in consumer credit outstanding and residential housing starts confirms broad-based demand recovery, which lifts cyclical equity earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain flat or negative during periods where both RSAFS and HOUST expand simultaneously, or joint proxy rank IC falls below 0.05 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects tightening supply constraints and rising capacity utilization, which expands corporate pricing power and profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive SPX returns during identified supply bottlenecks, or forward returns show no statistical difference between high and low INDPRO growth regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent signals economic stagnation, which reduces top-line revenue growth expectations and compresses equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of sub-1.5% GDP growth episodes, or the signal exhibits no predictive power in walk-forward backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Steady monthly payroll growth sustains consumer spending flows and supports corporate earnings trajectories, providing a persistent positive drift for equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes show no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous elevated inflation and restrictive monetary policy compress real yields and trigger valuation repricing, forcing institutional deleveraging and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally or remain flat when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS reach high levels simultaneously, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample prediction accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.6 million units annually signal robust construction demand and consumer confidence, which drives positive forward returns for rate-sensitive and cyclical equity sectors over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX underperforms its historical average when HOUST sustains levels above 1.6M for consecutive months, or forward returns show no significant premium during high-start regimes.
